title: Undercut/overcut value proxy from simplified stop-now vs stop-later simulation
summary: Pit-stop research and strategy references frame timing as comparing the value of stopping now versus extending the stint under degradation and pit-loss tradeoffs.
rationale: Pit-stop research and strategy references frame timing as comparing the value of stopping now versus extending the stint under degradation and pit-loss tradeoffs. A one-step policy-value feature is more faithful to race strategy than thresholding TyreLife alone.
implementation_hint: Build features that approximate delta race time for stop now, stop next lap, or extend two laps using current degradation rate, fresh-tyre finish margin, and simple pit-loss constants inferred per race type. Add interaction features with position and position-change to capture undercut pressure.
expected_effect: Should sharpen predictions near the real decision boundary, especially late-stint rows where several compounds are still feasible but timing matters.
risk: Pit-loss constants are unobserved and noisy; bad approximations can add variance. Keep it as a feature block, not a hard rule engine.
